Structural System Summary

Primary structural system: Wood framing and load-bearing CMU walls. Structural steel is limited to isolated columns, lintels, and beams. Lateral force-resisting system: Wood shear walls and CMU shear walls. Floor system: Engineered wood I-joists with 3/4" T&G sheathing. Roof system: Pre-engineered wood trusses with 3/4" T&G sheathing. Foundation interface: Concrete spread footings and strip footings. Special conditions: AESS is not mentioned. Galvanizing is required for all bearing plates. Trellis structure noted.
Approximate Tonnage Estimate

Primary structural steel: 2.97 t Secondary and miscellaneous steel: 0.96 t Combined project total: 3.93 t Basis: Member take-off from framing plans S1.01, S1.02, S3.06 and schedules on S0.02. Lintel lengths estimated based on typical opening sizes as architectural drawings are not provided.

10. DRAWING PACKAGE ASSESSMENT
Drawing Completeness Assessment
Caption: Fabrication readiness evaluation across eight key drawing quality indicators.
Indicator | Finding | Blocks Fabrication Start
Revision and Approval Stage | Construction Documents are issued, but one sheet has a conflicting date. | NO
Connection Design Completeness | CRITICAL: No typical connection details are provided for beam-to-beam or beam-to-column joints. | YES
Dimensional Clarity | Column lengths and exact beam locations require clarification. Plans are readable but lack sufficient dimensions for detailing. | YES
Scope Definition | Steel scope is identifiable but interfaces with other trades (CMU, Wood) are not detailed. | NO
Specification Availability | General notes provide most material grades and basic specifications. | NO
Cross-Sheet Consistency | One sheet has a conflicting date. Otherwise, grids and member marks appear consistent. | NO
AISC and AWS Compliance Indicators | General compliance with AISC/AWS is specified, but lack of connection details prevents verification. | YES
Anchor Bolt Data Completeness | CRITICAL: Embedment and projection are missing for most anchor bolts. | YES
Fabrication Start Recommendation: HOLD
Reason: Critical dimensional information, anchor bolt data, and all connection design details are missing from the provided drawings, making it impossible to begin shop detailing.

12. READY-TO-SEND RFI PACKAGE
CRITICAL RFIs — must be answered before modeling begins:
RFI-001 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: S1.02 — Steel Connection Details Priority: CRITICAL Blocking: YES

Question: Drawings do not provide typical connection details for steel beam-to-column or beam-to-beam framing (e.g., at Grid B/3 connecting W16x36 to HSS6x6 C6 column). Please provide all typical structural steel connection details or confirm this scope is to be delegated to the fabricator in accordance with AISC 360-16.
Expected Response Format: Stamped and signed connection detail sheets or written confirmation that connection design is delegated.
RFI-002 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: S0.02 — Anchor Bolt Schedule Priority: CRITICAL Blocking: YES

Question: The Steel Column Schedule on sheet S0.02 is missing critical anchor bolt information. Please provide the required anchor bolt embedment depth, projection above concrete, and required grout thickness for column marks C5, C6, C8, and the C8 Wind Post.
Expected Response Format: An updated and complete anchor bolt schedule or markups on sheet S0.02.
RFI-003 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: S0.02, S1.01 — Steel Column Lengths Priority: CRITICAL Blocking: YES

Question: No finished lengths or top-of-steel elevations are provided for steel columns C5, C6, and C8. Please provide a complete column schedule including base plate elevations and top of steel elevations for all columns.
Expected Response Format: An updated column schedule or elevations dimensioned on framing plans.
URGENT RFIs — must be answered within the first week of work:
RFI-004 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: S0.01 — Structural Bolting Specification Priority: URGENT Blocking: NO

Question: General Note 1.F on sheet S0.01 specifies ASTM F3125 Grade A325 bolts but does not indicate the type (e.g., A325-N with threads in shear plane, or A325-X with threads excluded). Please clarify which bolt type is required for all bolted connections.
Expected Response Format: Written confirmation of required bolt type (N or X).
RFI-005 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: S0.02, S1.02 — Non-Standard HSS Sizes Priority: URGENT Blocking: NO

Question: The following member sizes are not listed in the AISC Steel Construction Manual: HSS6x6x5/8, HSS8x6x3/8, HSS18x6x3/8, HSS9x5x3/8. Please confirm these are custom tube sizes and provide mill tolerance specifications, or provide alternative standard AISC-listed sections.
Expected Response Format: Written confirmation or a revised member schedule.
STANDARD RFIs — must be answered before drawing release for approval:
RFI-006 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: Drawing Set — Conflicting Issue Dates Priority: STANDARD Blocking: NO

Question: Sheet S2.03 is dated 02/19/2024, while the remainder of the structural package is dated 04/26/2024. Please confirm which issue date is current for S2.03 and advise if any revisions are pending.
Expected Response Format: Written confirmation or a re-issued drawing sheet.
RFI-007 To: Engineer of Record (Structural) Re: S0.01 — Steel Finish Specification Priority: STANDARD Blocking: NO

Question: The drawings note that all bearing plates shall be galvanized. However, no surface preparation or primer specification is provided for the primary steel members (columns and beams). Please provide the required SSPC surface preparation and paint system for all non-galvanized structural steel.
Expected Response Format: Written specification for surface prep and primer (e.g., SSPC-SP2, Universal Alkyd Primer).
--- Total RFIs issued: 7   Critical: 3   Urgent: 2   Standard: 2
